title: hyptis crassipes
intro: Hyptis crassipes, also known as Mexican tea or bush tea, is a perennial shrub native to Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hyptis crassipes include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Hyptis crassipes can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
